teachingHint: Your child learned 'oo' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the long /oo/ sound (as in 'moon') in more advanced words: s-p-oo-n → spoon, s-ch-oo-l → school. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-p-oo-n → 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound. Blend: sp-oo-n → spoon
s-ch-oo-l → 'ch' is a digraph, 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound. Blend: s-ch-oo-l → school
t-oo-th → 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound, 'th' is a digraph. Blend: t-oo-th → tooth
p-r-oo-f → 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound. Blend: pr-oo-f → proof

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Oh' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'h' is silent. We say 'oh'. Learn it by sight.

story: Oh! I left my spoon at school! I need proof that I brushed each tooth. Can you see my clean tooth?

teachingHint: Your child learned 'oo' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the long /oo/ sound in longer, more advanced words: b-a-ll-oo-n → balloon, s-c-oo-t-er → scooter. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-c-oo-t-er → 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound, 'er' is a digraph. Blend: sc-oo-t-er → scooter
b-a-ll-oo-n → 'll' is a digraph, 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound. Blend: b-a-ll-oo-n → balloon
c-ar-t-oo-n → 'ar' is a digraph, 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound. Blend: c-ar-t-oo-n → cartoon
m-u-sh-r-oo-m → 'sh' is a digraph, 'oo' makes the long /oo/ sound. Blend: m-u-sh-r-oo-m → mushroom

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Their' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'ei' makes an /air/ sound. We say 'their'. Learn it by sight.

story: Look at their cartoon balloon! It floats past the scooter and over the mushroom patch. What a sight!

teachingHint: Your child learned 'oo' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the short /oo/ sound (as in 'book') in more advanced words: sh-oo-k → shook, b-r-oo-k → brook. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: s-t-oo-d → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound (like 'book'). Blend: st-oo-d → stood
sh-oo-k → 'sh' is a digraph, 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: sh-oo-k → shook
b-r-oo-k → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: br-oo-k → brook
h-oo-k → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: h-oo-k → hook

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Looked' has the short /oo/ sound in 'look' plus '-ed'. The '-ed' says /t/. Learn it by sight.

story: I looked at the brook and stood still. A fish shook on the hook! What a catch!

teachingHint: Your child learned 'oo' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the short /oo/ sound in more advanced words: c-r-oo-k-e-d → crooked, w-oo-l → wool. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: c-r-oo-k-e-d → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: cr-oo-k-e-d → crooked
h-oo-d → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: h-oo-d → hood
w-oo-l → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: w-oo-l → wool
s-t-oo-d → 'oo' makes the short /oo/ sound. Blend: st-oo-d → stood

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Could' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'ou' makes a short /oo/ sound and the 'l' is silent. Learn it by sight.

story: I could see a crooked path. I stood and pulled up my hood. The wool coat kept me warm in the wind.

teachingHint: Your child learned 'ow' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the loud /ow/ sound (as in 'cow') in more advanced words: c-r-ow-d → crowd, f-l-ow-er → flower. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: c-r-ow-d → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound. Blend: cr-ow-d → crowd
b-r-ow-n → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound. Blend: br-ow-n → brown
f-l-ow-er → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound, 'er' is a digraph. Blend: fl-ow-er → flower
p-ow-er → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound, 'er' is a digraph. Blend: p-ow-er → power

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'People' doesn't follow normal rules. The 'eo' makes an /ee/ sound and the 'le' says /l/. Learn it by sight.

story: A crowd of people stood by the brown flower. It had the power to grow so tall and bright!

teachingHint: Your child learned 'ow' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the loud /ow/ sound in more advanced words: t-ow-er → tower, sh-ow-er → shower. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: t-ow-er → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound, 'er' is a digraph. Blend: t-ow-er → tower
sh-ow-er → 'sh' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound, 'er' is a digraph. Blend: sh-ow-er → shower
v-ow-e-l → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound. Blend: v-ow-e-l → vowel
c-r-ow-d → 'ow' makes the loud /ow/ sound. Blend: cr-ow-d → crowd

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Called' has 'a' making an /or/ sound and '-ed' says /d/. Learn it by sight.

story: Mr Fox called from the tower. A shower of rain fell on the crowd. Can you spot the vowel in each word?

teachingHint: Your child learned 'ow' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the slow /ow/ sound (as in 'snow') in more advanced words: f-o-ll-ow → follow, w-i-n-d-ow → window. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: f-o-ll-ow → 'll' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: f-o-ll-ow → follow
w-i-n-d-ow → 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: w-i-n-d-ow → window
sh-a-d-ow → 'sh' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: sh-a-d-ow → shadow
e-l-b-ow → 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: e-l-b-ow → elbow

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Asked' has '-ed' that says /t/. The 'a' says /ah/. Learn it by sight.

story: Mrs Bee asked me to follow her shadow. I bumped my elbow on the window! Ouch!

teachingHint: Your child learned 'ow' in Phase 3. Now they're reviewing the slow /ow/ sound in more advanced words: r-ai-n-b-ow → rainbow, p-i-ll-ow → pillow. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: r-ai-n-b-ow → 'ai' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: r-ai-n-b-ow → rainbow
y-e-ll-ow → 'll' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: y-e-ll-ow → yellow
p-i-ll-ow → 'll' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: p-i-ll-ow → pillow
f-o-ll-ow → 'll' is a digraph, 'ow' makes the slow /oh/ sound. Blend: f-o-ll-ow → follow

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! 'Mr' is short for 'Mister'. We say 'mister' but write 'Mr'. Learn it by sight.

story: Mr Fox saw a yellow rainbow from his pillow. He got up to follow it across the meadow!
